paper_title: Breeding and Comparative Analysis of Tetraploid Rice Huaduo1

methods.method_name: Breeding Procedure of New Tetraploid Rice Huaduo1
methods.description: This method describes the breeding process used to develop a new tetraploid rice line, Huaduo1, from a hybrid of two autotetraploid rice plants. The process involved generating hybrids, self-crossing, and evaluating agronomic traits to overcome sterility in hybrids.

steps.description: A hybrid of two autotetraploid rice plants, Jackson-4x (T45-4x) and 96025-4x (T44-4x), was generated in 2004, and the F1 hybrid plants were harvested and continuously self-crossed until F5 in 2007.
steps.searchable_summary: generate and self-cross hybrid rice plants to F5 generation

steps.description: One line with more than 80% seed setting was found in that year, and a new tetraploid rice line, Huaduo1 (H1), was developed in 2009 and registered for PVP in China in 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: select and register new rice line with high seed setting

steps.description: To evaluate the ability to overcome the sterility of hybrids in H1, we developed hybrids with gene interactions in the pollen sterility loci, Sa, Sb, and Sc, using H1 crossed with Taichung 65-4x and its pollen sterility isogenic lines.
steps.searchable_summary: develop hybrids to test sterility overcoming ability

methods.method_name: Comparative Transcriptome Analysis of Huaduo1 and Its Parents in Meiosis
methods.description: This method involves conducting transcriptome analysis to explore gene expression variations associated with high pollen fertility in Huaduo1 compared to its parents. Differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified and categorized.

steps.description: We collected the meiotic anthers of H1 and its parents and detected their gene expression to identify the possible DEGs of high pollen fertility.
steps.searchable_summary: collect meiotic anthers and detect gene expression

steps.description: We divided the DEGs into three groups based on their origin derived from the two parents or non-parental variations.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize DEGs into groups based on origin

steps.description: Using these comparison groups, we identified a total of 3896 genes that were differentially expressed (twofold at a p value of <0.05) between H1 and its parents in meiosis.
steps.searchable_summary: identify differentially expressed genes with statistical significance

steps.description: We categorized both the up-and downregulated genes using Cluster 3.0 software and obtained an overview of the transcriptome relationships.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize and visualize gene expression patterns

steps.description: Gene ontology (GO) analysis was conducted to annotate the common up-and down-regulated DEGs between H1 and its parents.
